risk_title: Governance Fragility and Institutional Bottlenecks Delaying Project Implementation
risk_summary: State administrative bottlenecks and delays in obtaining state approvals are impeding project implementation timelines. The document indicates that the Ogun State Project Financial Management Unit's support is needed to improve capacity and navigate administrative obstacles, suggesting governance fragility affecting development actor coordination.
evidence_quote: The Ogun State Project Financial Management Unit's support is needed to improve the internal auditor's capacity and navigate the PIU's administrative bottlenecks.

risk_title: Tension Between Federal and State Government on Project Restructuring
risk_summary: The amended Financing Agreement signed by the World Bank on June 28, 2024, remains unsigned by the Federal Republic of Nigeria as of August 30, 2024, creating a governance impasse. This delay in federal countersignature is blocking project restructuring and subsequent subsidiary loan agreement amendments, indicating tension between federal and state development implementation.
evidence_quote: The amended financing agreement signed by the World Bank (June 28, 2024) is awaiting countersignature from the Federal Government of Nigeria.

risk_title: Potential Project Activity Cancellations Due to Implementation Delays
risk_summary: A second restructuring is planned for October 2024 to cancel activities if key milestones are not met by September 2024. This reflects governance fragility and implementation capacity constraints that may force abandonment of critical development objectives, including rural road rehabilitation and technical college upgrades.
evidence_quote: The Project may require a second restructuring in October 2024 to cancel activities if key milestones are not met by September 2024. Significant acceleration in implementation is needed to complete project activities by closing.

risk_title: Delays in State Approvals Blocking Procurement and Service Delivery
risk_summary: Multiple procurement processes are delayed due to lack of state approvals, including pre-qualification evaluation reports pending since April 2024 and consultancy service approvals. These delays are directly affecting service delivery to farmers and beneficiaries, indicating governance capacity constraints.
evidence_quote: The following are still pending approval with the State – (i) the recruitment of consultancy services for the development of feasibility studies and ESMP; (ii) pre-qualification evaluation report of contractors (pending since April 2024); (iii) consultancy services for the preparation of the ESMPs for post-harvest processing facilities and poultry pens in four zones.

risk_title: Payment Delays to Service Providers Affecting Farmer Livelihoods
risk_summary: Delays in reimbursement payments to service and input providers are slowing delivery of critical services to farmers, creating livelihood pressures. This reflects governance and financial management fragility affecting vulnerable populations dependent on project support.
evidence_quote: However, challenges slowing progress in this area include (i) delays in payments to reimburse the service/input providers, which in turn delays delivery of services; (ii) insufficient service providers to meet the farmer demand.
